_README: The Guardian's Library — your shelf. Add a resource by appending one object to `resources`: { id (unique), type: 'video'|'book'|'essay', title, by (author/channel), url (paste the link; leave '' and the card will say 'link pending'), note (one line, optional), tags (which drift patterns it speaks to) }. The Counsel pattern-detector picks the best-tagged resource for the drift it sees in your witnessed checkpoints. No code change needed — just edit this file. The starter entries below are real titles with the url left blank for you to fill from a source you trust.

patterns.evening-drift: The late-night high-risk window opening up

resources.title: Morning light, wake time, and the circadian system
resources.note: Why a fixed wake + light beats a fixed bedtime — sell it to yourself as a performance lever.

resources.title: Atomic Habits — “never miss twice”
resources.note: One miss is noise; two in a row is the start of a new habit. Hold the second day.

resources.title: Urge surfing — riding the wave instead of fighting it
resources.note: The urge crests and passes if you don't act. For the late-night window.

resources.title: Discipline Equals Freedom
resources.note: When motivation is gone, the protocol carries you. Short, blunt, on-voice.

resources.title: Stimulus control: the phone leaves the bedroom
resources.note: Bed = sleep only. The single highest-leverage change for the night cutoff.
